notes: Spec asserts: ready → working → ready when a turn ends with a terminal assistant text message and no tool call. Unlike Claude Code's explicit stop_reason: end_turn, Gemini emits NO explicit end-of-turn marker — the parser INFERS turn_done from a final text-only 'gemini' message with non-empty content and no open tool calls (parser.go:209), which makes IsAgentDone()=true via the primary path and classifier rule 2b fires working→ready. Overlaps intentionally with basic-turn — this cell pins the end-signal mechanism specifically. Recorded with the INTERACTIVE gemini-cli driver (gemini -y under tmux: send + wait_turn + 8s trailing sleep), NOT headless gemini -p, which exits at turn completion before the daemon observes the terminal ready edge. turn_end_terminal anchors to turn_start (not session_birth) so the matcher picks the post-turn ready, not the birth ready. The model/cost/tokens observations are checked via the observations vector below; observations.model ('gemini-3-flash-preview') is the live-verified default boot model and is PROVISIONAL — record settles it; a different live default is a string update, not a daemon bug.
source: .specs/agent-scenarios.md → Feature: Turn end via terminal text

text: Session appears in ready shortly after launch
Launcher PID bound by cwd shortly after launch
Final text-only 'gemini' message with no tool calls is reclassified turn_done → working→ready (IsAgentDone primary path + rule 2b); state never entered waiting
